title: nezera cathartica
side_effects: Nezera cathartica can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, and irregular heartbeat, which can be caused by excessive laxative properties. Prolonged use can lead to malabsorption of essential nutrients, resulting in deficiencies and fatigue. In some cases, misuse of Nezera cathartica can also cause stomach cramps, diarrhea, and vomiting, especially in individuals with pre-existing gastrointestinal conditions. Additionally, overuse can disrupt the body's natural bowel function, leading to long-term dependence on the plant's laxative effects.
